sast_review.reason: The subprocess.run call uses a list constructed from user-supplied 'command' and 'env' values without any sanitization. The command string is appended directly to the Docker argument list, allowing injection of arbitrary Docker flags/arguments (e.g., '--rm -v /:/host bash').
Importing subprocess is flagged because it can be used to execute arbitrary commands. In this code, it is indeed used unsafely (see B603), so the warning is justified.

raw.summary: The code passes the user-supplied command string directly to `docker run` as the last argument. Because the command is appended after the image name, an attacker can inject arbitrary Docker arguments by starting the command with `-` (e.g., `-v /:/host bash`). The role check via the `X-User-Roles` header is trivially bypassed by an attacker. Both SAST findings are true positives.
